description_long: This is a vintage metal kettle with a wide, slightly flared base and a gently tapering body. The kettle features a long, curved side-mounted handle made of metal, which is attached at two points: one near the rim and the other extending outward from the body. The lid is flat with a rounded knob at the center, and it appears to be removable. The spout is short and subtly integrated into the rim, designed for controlled pouring. The surface of the kettle is matte and shows signs of age, with a dark, weathered patina and no visible decorative patterns.
description: A vintage metal kettle with a wide base, curved side handle, flat removable lid with knob, and a short spout. The surface is matte and weathered, with no visible decorations.
description_view_0: The kettle is seen from an angle highlighting the long, curved handle and the flat lid with a central knob.
description_view_1: This view shows the handle extending directly toward the viewer, obscuring part of the lid and spout.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the kettle is visible, with the handle extending away and the spout partially visible.
description_view_3: A rear angle showing the handle attachment and the back of the kettle, with the knob on the lid clearly visible.
